title: Erasing instance data upon app uninstall
excerpt: Hi, while working on the marketplace apps, we realised that when using the instance storage, we need to listen to the “install” webhook in order to erase the instance data right after the installation (if the app is unis…

posts.author: Konstantin_CS (Konstantin Frolov)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, while working on the marketplace apps, we realised that when using the instance storage, we need to listen to the “install” webhook in order to erase the instance data right after the installation (if the app is unistalled and then re-installed again).

It would be great to be able delete the instance data after uninstallation (without needing to reinstall). Any thoughts on this are welcome. Thanks!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
I believe monday has said that if using monday storage (stored on their system) the expectation is you leave the data so its available if they reinstall the app. That there isn’t a requirement to delete the data on uninstall if using their storage.

Monday Storage Persistence After Uninstalling the App monday Apps & Developers
Dear Community, I hope this message finds you all in good spirits. I would like to inquire about the fate of account Storage once a user decides to uninstall the app. Are the data wiped out entirely upon uninstallation, or does Monday.com retain any portion of this data for a certain period or under specific circumstances? Thank you all for your contributions and support. I eagerly await your insights and feedback.

posts.author: Konstantin_CS (Konstantin Frolov)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing the link, Cody. Interestingly enough, I have also heard the opposite thing - that the review team has the requirements that reinstalled app using monday storage should be “fresh” as it is a brand new installation. Maybe requirements have changed since Feb. I’ll try to double-check this and report.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I will be honest, I never take the word of the review team for anything. You need to go above them to find out for sure - they appear to often make things up as they go, or are unable to apply requirements appropriately in differing contexts.

posts.author: AnnaPor (Anna Pormanov)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Cody! Anna here from the review team.
Is there a specific reason your’e saying that? What was the exact instance on an exact review of an app of yours where we delivered the wrong information? Would love to know!

Other that that, i can ensure you we have a very specific review process, that seems to be working well, and do not “make things up as we go”, a great evidence of that would be the growing and successful marketplace monday is building

posts.author: Konstantin_CS (Konstantin Frolov)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Anna, we are actually planning to submit couple of the apps in the coming weeks, so may I ask on the topic of this thread - when the app is uninstalled, is the global storage supposed to be cleared out from our side or not? So that if the customer reinstalls the app, are they supposed to find their data there. Thank you!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Making it up as they go is in reference to each reviewer applying requirements according to their interpretation at that moment in time. I don’t know if we’re now unburdened by what has been with the passage of time though.

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wiping the app storage on app uninstall is an appalling idea.

If the app was uninstalled by mistake and then reinstalled after the mistake is discovered, it would kill all work done and make customers angry.

Don’t take my word for it, this is already happening on other platforms. People are not impressed.

Far better to have an automated job which deletes all data, say 30 days after the uninstall has taken place. If the app is reinstalled within the 30 days, then nothing happens, but if the app is missing for 30 days, then the data is removed.

This is something that monday.com will have to implement as once the app is removed, 3rd party vendors cannot get at the data.

Importantly, the policy needs to be stated and documented in help docs.
